vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but requires significant revision based on visual evidence. P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is present. P1 (String of Nickels) is present. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are largely absent or represented by necrotic remnants.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No viable Mexican Mint visible).
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Pothos, black pot, white rabbit anchor).
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No viable Silver Guest visible).

plant_audit.P1: Stable. The succulent foliage remains consistent throughout the sequence.
plant_audit.P3: Stable. The Pothos leaf and the white rabbit anchor remain in situ. The necrotic leaf observed in the earliest image persists as a dried remnant.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Pot: Degrading. The central black pot shows a single, thin, vertical sprout that appears to be struggling; no evidence of the registered species (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) is present.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp; no signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshell fragments in the P2/P4 pot, acting as a potential soil amendment or mulch.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-3: Minimal change; the Pothos leaf in P3 remains static.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: The sprout in the P2/P4 pot has undergone a slight postural shift, appearing more upright but lacking secondary leaf development.

visual_health_inference.P2_P4: Critical/Failure (specimens are not thriving; potential misidentification or total loss of original occupants).

anomalies: Eggshell fragments in P2/P4 pot (New Intervention).
Unidentified thin sprout in P2/P4 pot (New Introduction/Volunteer).
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of stasis with high mortality in the P2/P4 sector. The Pothos (P3) and String of Nickels (P1) are holding their position, though the Pothos shows signs of previous senescence. The most significant development is the emergence of an uncatalogued sprout in the P2/P4 pot, which is currently the only sign of life in that container, suggesting the original specimens have been lost.
